Alright, JB in the house, the wolf is here. Time for your throwback Thursday daily motivation slash skill string. This was easy guys, I have so much info on prospecting. I picked an awesome clip for you, check it out right now and I'll see you on the other side. When you're done asking your questions and you want to transition from qualifying into the body of your presentation, you want to have what I call an anchor phrase, which is something that basically you could say again and again and again, it's very generic and it just works every time. Now for me, I always use, you know, listen, well based on everything you've just said to me, what I have here is definitely a perfect fit for you. I'll say that again, so my generic phrase is, listen, well based on everything you just said to me, the program we have here is definitely a perfect fit for you. That's just simply my anchor, it allows me to transition from qualifying into the body of my presentation and the way I do that is by saying, okay, so real quick, let me tell you about my product, it's A, B and boom, I'm in the body of my presentation. Okay, one last recap here, okay, A, I want you to write your questions down, make sure you come with the perfect questions for your industry. It's A, B, I want you to make sure you figure out the precise tonalities that allow you to ask these questions and be perceived in the way you need to be perceived, which is the trusted advisor as opposed to the grand inquisitor. Three, write down the order in which you're going to ask these questions and again, big picture to small picture, right? D, remember that when you ask a question, you'll listen to the answer, you make mental notes and you don't try to solve the pain right there. You make mental notes for later on and also you might ask additional questions based on what they said. You don't try to solve their pain, right? And then finally at the end, when you're done, you've asked all the questions and you've gotten the keys to the kingdom essentially, they've told you what they need exactly, where their pain is exactly, you kind of get a really good sense of what this person's all about then and this is only if you think that you can truly help the person. If your product suits their needs, they can afford it, it's going to really help them out, right? Then you say, well, let me say this, you know, based on everything you just said to me, what we have here is definitely a perfect fit for you. Real quick, let me tell you exactly what we have and then boom, boom, boom, we're in our main presentation. Okay? And that's essentially the keys to the kingdom of straight line prospecting. So write your questions down in the right order, the right tone now and if you do this and manage your sales funnel, your charts, all this sort of, these basic tools that just allow you essentially to tip the odds in your favor. They give you one leg up on everybody else around you because A, you're not going to be spending any time talking to unqualified people, right? Most people get eliminated out of the pipeline, the look you lose, right? And the people who really just would drag them in the first place, they're out of the pipeline, right? And B, the people who are in the pipeline and who you are talking to, they're qualified in a way that builds on the rapport you already created in that, you know, initial pop of sharpest attack, enthusiastic sale, expert in your field, right? You build on that and you get yourself at a time you're done, it's such tight lockstep rapport that when you actually do offer the solution, they're like, well, wow, they just, they're open ears, they're zeroed into you. And that's why I have such a high closing rate because before I even open up my mouth or open the solution, they're like, wow, this is a guy I connect with, I trust this guy, he really cares about me and boom, when I come in and do my stuff on the back end, meaning go through a straight line pitch, lower action threshold, add on pain, do my looping, I truly can close anyone who's closable and so will you.
